commit.message:
Expand: rich sensor/button/gesture APIs, program management, DeviceType/Gesture enums

New Hub methods (all tested against simulator):
- color/reflection/ambient/raw_rgb(port) - color sensor via eval_python
- force/is_pressed(port) - force sensor via eval_python
- distance(port) - ultrasonic sensor via eval_python
- motor_position/speed/was_interrupted/is_stalled(port) - motor queries
- left/right_button_pressed/was_pressed() - hub button queries
- get_gesture/was_gesture - IMU gesture detection
- temperature/charger_connected - hub environment
- wait_for_button(button, timeout) - blocking button wait
- run_slot/stop_slot - program slot aliases
- run_python_program(code, slot) - upload + run shorthand
- list_slots/display_number/sound_play_note/motor_run_until_stalled
- get_port_info() - port→device_id map from DeviceNotification

New enums: DeviceType (LPF2 IDs: MOTOR_MEDIUM/LARGE/SMALL, COLOR/DISTANCE/FORCE_SENSOR),
           Gesture (NONE/SHAKE/FREEFALL/TAPPED/DOUBLE_TAPPED)

Simulator: ConsoleHandler handles 15+ new eval_python patterns;
           HubState gains button_left/right, gesture, hub_temperature,
           press_button/release_button/trigger_gesture helpers

tunnel.py: display_number, sound_play_note, motor_run_until_stalled,
           display_animate command builders

eval_python fix: split multi-line responses by newline before parsing

New examples: example_sensors.py, example_program.py, example_events.py
Tests: 44/44 pass (up from 24/24)

commit.message:
fix(smAgent): include target agent customParams in encoded_config

When SM triggers agents via old-style paths (agents/pr_review.json),
buildEncodedConfig was only forwarding configPath in customParams.
If dmtools replaces customParams entirely (vs merging), autoStartRework,
autoStartReworkConfigFile, targetRepository, etc. were lost — causing
the SM to re-trigger rework separately with the wrong (generic) path.

Fix: resolve project-specific agent JSON (ai_teammate/<projectKey>/pr_review.json)
and include its customParams as the base in encoded_config. configPath from
effectiveConfig still overrides to ensure the project config is always found.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 5c: migrate setter lookups in property assignment/read to helpers

- VmFunction/VmClosure setter lookup: make_setter_key + .get() → lookup_setter (2 sites)
- VmObject setter-only check (read): make_setter_key + contains_key → has_setter (2 sites)
- VmArray setter-only check (read): make_setter_key + contains_key → has_setter
- VmFunction key_exists check: contains_key(&setter_key) → has_setter
- Eliminated 6 make_setter_key calls from vm.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add AWS SigV4 authentication support (#10)

Add three authentication modes for Amazon Bedrock:

1. Bearer token (existing): Bedrock API key via 'key' config
2. SigV4 with explicit credentials: AWS access key + secret key + optional session token
3. Default AWS credential chain: IAM roles, instance profiles, ECS task roles, environment variables

This enables enterprise users who cannot use API keys to authenticate
via IAM roles and temporary credentials from STS.

Uses Aws\Signature\SignatureV4 as Guzzle middleware to sign requests.
Backward compatible — existing API key users are unaffected.

commit.message:
feat(SM-15): Wire BindingAccumulator into processCallsFromExtracted for cross-file return type propagation (#763)

* Initial plan

* Initial setup - Phase 9 BindingAccumulator cross-file return type wiring

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/7cee6490-090d-4714-8cb5-a704168ff47a

* feat(SM-15): wire BindingAccumulator into processCallsFromExtracted for Phase 9 cross-file return type propagation

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/7cee6490-090d-4714-8cb5-a704168ff47a

* fix(SM-15): address all PR #763 review findings

Performance (R1)
- Changed _fileScopeByFile from Map<string, [string,string][]> to
  Map<string, Map<string,string>>. fileScopeGet(filePath, name) is
  now O(1) — replaces the O(n) linear scan + defensive-copy alloc
  that ran once per ConstructorBinding entry. fileScopeEntries()
  reconstructs tuples from Map.entries() for backward compat.
- Updated finalize() dev-mode invariant to compare deduplicated Map
  size rather than raw array length (Map.set deduplicates same-name).

Lifecycle (R2)
- Documented that Phase 9 intentionally reads pre-finalize because
  finalize() cannot move before both the worker consumer (line 984)
  AND the sequential-path writer (line 1061). Pre-finalize reads are
  safe because finalize() is write-lock-only with no side effects.
  Replaced the ambiguous "populated but not yet finalized" comment
  with the full lifecycle ordering explanation.

Sequential-path parity (R3)
- Wired bindingAccumulator into processCalls at line 797 (sequential
  path) so verifyConstructorBindings gets the Phase 9 fallback.
- Added bindingAccumulator parameter to processAssignmentsFromExtracted
  signature and wired it at the pipeline.ts call site (line 1026).
- Both paths now produce identical Phase 9 behavior for the same code.

Tracking comments (R4)
- Added "Overlapping mechanism (N of 3)" cross-references at:
  1. buildImportedReturnTypes (~line 109)
  2. collectExportedBindings (~line 168)
  3. Phase 9 fallback in verifyConstructorBindings (~line 563)
  Each links to the other two and notes future unification.

Language coverage (R5)
- Added 5 new Phase 9 integration test suites in cross-file-binding.test.ts:
  JavaScript, C++, C#, PHP, Ruby. Each uses the existing fixture
  directories and asserts getUser() → User → user.save() resolves.
  Total cross-file binding tests: 52 (was 37).

Quality asymmetry (R6)
- Added inline comment at the Phase 9 fallback noting worker-path
  entries are Tier 0/1 only and that binding accuracy is structurally
  lower for large repos where the worker path dominates.

Tests (+21 new)
- 6 fileScopeGet unit tests (happy path, unknown file/name, mixed
  scopes, post-dispose, duplicate varName last-write-wins)
- 15 integration tests across 5 new language suites

Verification
- tsc --noEmit clean
- 3147 unit tests pass (+6 new)
- 52 cross-file binding integration tests pass (+15 new)
- 1766 resolver integration tests pass
- Zero regressions

Plan: docs/plans/2026-04-10-001-fix-sm15-review-findings-plan.md
Review: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/pull/763#issuecomment-4220354242

* fix(SM-15): gate accumulator fallback on resolution tier and fix sequential file-order dependency

Two Codex adversarial reviews identified medium-severity bugs in the Phase 9
BindingAccumulator fallback:

1. Local-first violation: the fallback fired regardless of whether ctx.resolve()
   found same-file candidates, letting an imported callee shadow a local one
   and produce false CALLS edges. Fixed by gating on tiered.tier !== 'same-file'
   and callableDefs.length <= 1.

2. Sequential file-order dependency: processCalls flushed and verified per-file,
   so consumer files processed before their providers missed accumulator bindings.
   Fixed by splitting into a flush pre-pass (all files) then a resolution loop,
   mirroring the worker path's "all appends before any reads" pattern.

Also adds 11 consumer-before-provider integration test fixtures (one per
supported language) and 4 unit tests for tier gating edge cases.

* refactor(SM-15): eliminate duplicated prepare logic in processCalls two-pass split

Replace the duplicated pre-pass + legacy-path code (parse → query → heritage
→ TypeEnv → exports) with a single preparation loop followed by a resolution
loop. Both paths now share the same preparation code — the only conditional
is the accumulator flush.

Side benefit: globalParentMap is now fully populated before any resolution
runs, improving cross-file isSubclassOf accuracy regardless of file order.

Net -118 lines (226 removed, 108 added).

* fix(SM-15): address PR #763 third-pass review findings

1. Update stale dispose() JSDoc — remove forward-reference to Phase 9
   wiring that is now complete; document actual consumers.

2. Add processAssignmentsFromExtracted Phase 9 unit test — verifies the
   accumulator fallback produces ACCESSES write edges when the SymbolTable
   has no returnType for the callee.

feat: PA Rule Explorer for TMPPM prior auth rules (#636)

* feat: scaffold PA Rule Explorer Blazor page for TMPPM prior auth rules

Adds a new Compliance section to the portal with a PA Rule Explorer page
that queries TMPPM PA rules from Cosmos DB (MongoDB API). Supports search
by CPT/HCPCS code with autocomplete, category browsing by priority tier,
edition metadata with freshness indicators, and month-over-month diff view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move LINQ lambda out of Razor markup to fix Release build

The Razor compiler in Release mode misparses inline lambdas like
.Sum(c => c.ExtractedRuleCount) inside markup. Extract to a method.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply code review feedback on PA Rule Explorer

- PaRuleSearchBar: store autocomplete suggestions locally (fixes re-render bug)
- PaRuleSearchBar: replace div items with button[role=option]+aria-selected for a11y
- TmppmRuleQueryService: read DB/collection names from IConfiguration with defaults
- TmppmRuleQueryService: remove synchronous EnsureIndexes() from constructor
- TmppmIndexService: new BackgroundService for async index creation at startup
- ITmppmRuleQueryService/TmppmRuleQueryService: add state param to Search/GetByCategory
- PaRuleCard: fix GetTmppmUrl() comment to reflect actual behavior
- TmppmRuleQueryServiceTests: 17 unit tests for mapping logic and query behavior

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/aurelianware/cloudhealthoffice/sessions/8167168e-be01-4ce6-ae16-e23e2b6bd4b1

Co-authored-by: aurelianware <194855645+aurelianware@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add TMPPM ingestion service for PA Rule Explorer data pipeline

Console tool that downloads TMHP TMPPM chapter PDFs, extracts prior
authorization rules via PdfPig + regex parsing, and persists them to
Cosmos DB (MongoDB API) for the PA Rule Explorer page to query.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add TMPPM PA Rules page and extraction pipeline SVG

* fix: eliminate string interpolation from Razor markup for Release build

The Release Razor compiler misparses @($"...") inside MudChip content,
breaking brace tracking and failing to recognize the @code block. Replace
all inline expressions with plain method calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
